question: Do mitochondria play a role in remodelling lace plant leaves during programmed cell death?
expanded_query: Do mitochondria play a role in remodelling lace plant leaves during programmed cell death? mitochondrion Mitochondrion (cell structure) plants Viridiplantae Leaves, Plant Leaf Cell Death Process mitochondria lace plant programmed cell death cellular differentiation cell death
generated_answer: Reasoning: The lace plant (Aponogeton madagascariensis) produces perforations in its leaves through programmed cell death (PCD) [Context 21645374:0]. The leaves of the plant consist of a latticework of longitudinal and transverse veins enclosing areoles. PCD occurs in the cells at the center of these areoles and progresses outwards, stopping approximately five cells from the vasculature. The role of mitochondria during PCD has been recognized in animals; however, it has been less studied during PCD in plants.
raw_answer: Reasoning: The lace plant (Aponogeton madagascariensis) produces perforations in its leaves through programmed cell death (PCD). The leaves of the plant consist of a latticework of longitudinal and transverse veins enclosing areoles. PCD occurs in the cells at the center of these areoles and progresses outwards, stopping approximately five cells from the vasculature. The role of mitochondria during PCD has been recognized in animals; however, it has been less studied during PCD in plants.
reference_answer: Results depicted mitochondrial dynamics in vivo as PCD progresses within the lace plant, and highlight the correlation of this organelle with other organelles during developmental PCD. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first report of mitochondria and chloroplasts moving on transvacuolar strands to form a ring structure surrounding the nucleus during developmental PCD. Also, for the first time, we have shown the feasibility for the use of CsA in a whole plant system. Overall, our findings implicate the mitochondria as playing a critical and early role in developmentally regulated PCD in the lace plant.

details.sentence: Reasoning: The lace plant (Aponogeton madagascariensis) produces perforations in its leaves through programmed cell death (PCD).
The leaves of the plant consist of a latticework of longitudinal and transverse veins enclosing areoles.
PCD occurs in the cells at the center of these areoles and progresses outwards, stopping approximately five cells from the vasculature.
The role of mitochondria during PCD has been recognized in animals; however, it has been less studied during PCD in plants.

question: Landolt C and snellen e acuity: differences in strabismus amblyopia?
expanded_query: Landolt C and snellen e acuity: differences in strabismus amblyopia? Charts, Eye, Visual Acuity visual acuity chart age difference age differences Landolt C Snellen e amblyopia strabismus acuity visual perception visual field optometric optometry optometrist optometrist ophthalmology oph
generated_answer: Reasoning: The retrieved context provides information on the differences between Landolt C and Snellen E acuity in patients with strabismus amblyopia. The study included 100 patients (age 8 - 90 years, median 60.5 years) with various eye disorders, among them 39 with amblyopia due to strabismus [Context 16418930:1]. Charts with the Snellen E and the Landolt C (Precision Vision) which mimic the ETDRS charts were used to assess visual acuity [Context 16418930:1]. Three out of 5 optotypes per line had to be correctly identified, while wrong answers were monitored [Context 16418930:1]. In the group of patients, the eyes with the lower visual acuity, and the right eyes of the healthy subjects, were evaluated. The mean decimal values for LR and SE were 0.25 and 0.29 in the entire group and 0.14 and 0.16 for the eyes with strabismus amblyopia [Context 16418930:2]. The mean difference between LR and SE was 0.55 lines in the entire group and 0.55 lines for the eyes with strabismus amblyopia, with higher values of SE in both groups [Context 16418930:2]. The results of the other groups were similar with only small differences between LR and SE [Context 16418930:2].
raw_answer: Reasoning: The retrieved context provides information on the differences between Landolt C and Snellen E acuity in patients with strabismus amblyopia. The study included 100 patients (age 8 - 90 years, median 60.5 years) with various eye disorders, among them 39 with amblyopia due to strabismus. Charts with the Snellen E and the Landolt C (Precision Vision) which mimic the ETDRS charts were used to assess visual acuity. Three out of 5 optotypes per line had to be correctly identified, while wrong answers were monitored. In the group of patients, the eyes with the lower visual acuity, and the right eyes of the healthy subjects, were evaluated. The mean decimal values for LR and SE were 0.25 and 0.29 in the entire group and 0.14 and 0.16 for the eyes with strabismus amblyopia. The mean difference between LR and SE was 0.55 lines in the entire group and 0.55 lines for the eyes with strabismus amblyopia, with higher values of SE in both groups. The results of the other groups were similar with only small differences between LR and SE.
reference_answer: Using the charts described, there was only a slight overestimation of visual acuity by the Snellen E compared to the Landolt C, even in strabismus amblyopia. Small differences in the lower visual acuity range have to be considered.

details.sentence: Reasoning: The retrieved context provides information on the differences between Landolt C and Snellen E acuity in patients with strabismus amblyopia.

question: Syncope during bathing in infants, a pediatric form of water-induced urticaria?
expanded_query: Syncope during bathing in infants, a pediatric form of water-induced urticaria? FAINT attacks fainting Baths bathed Infant (person) INFANT Question: Is there a relationship between the use of statins and the risk of developing diabetes?
generated_answer: Reasoning: Eight infants aged 2 to 15 months were admitted during a period of 6 years; [Context 9488747:1] they suffered from similar maladies in the bath: on immersion, they became pale, hypotonic, still and unreactive; [Context 9488747:1] recovery took a few seconds after withdrawal from the bath and stimulation. Two diagnoses were initially considered: seizure or gastroesophageal reflux but this was doubtful. The hypothesis of an equivalent of aquagenic urticaria was then considered; [Context 9488747:1] as for patients with this disease, each infant's family contained members suffering from dermographism, maladies or eruption after exposure to water or sun. All six infants had dermographism. We found an increase in blood histamine levels after a trial bath in the two infants tested. The evolution of these "aquagenic maladies" was favourable after a 2-7 year follow-up.
raw_answer: Reasoning: Eight infants aged 2 to 15 months were admitted during a period of 6 years; they suffered from similar maladies in the bath: on immersion, they became pale, hypotonic, still and unreactive; recovery took a few seconds after withdrawal from the bath and stimulation. Two diagnoses were initially considered: seizure or gastroesophageal reflux but this was doubtful. The hypothesis of an equivalent of aquagenic urticaria was then considered; as for patients with this disease, each infant's family contained members suffering from dermographism, maladies or eruption after exposure to water or sun. All six infants had dermographism. We found an increase in blood histamine levels after a trial bath in the two infants tested. The evolution of these "aquagenic maladies" was favourable after a 2-7 year follow-up.
reference_answer: "Aquagenic maladies" could be a pediatric form of the aquagenic urticaria.

they suffered from similar maladies in the bath: on immersion, they became pale, hypotonic, still and unreactive;
recovery took a few seconds after withdrawal from the bath and stimulation.
Two diagnoses were initially considered: seizure or gastroesophageal reflux but this was doubtful.
The hypothesis of an equivalent of aquagenic urticaria was then considered;
as for patients with this disease, each infant's family contained members suffering from dermographism, maladies or eruption after exposure to water or sun.
All six infants had dermographism.

The evolution of these "aquagenic maladies" was favourable after a 2-7 year follow-up.

question: Are the long-term results of the transanal pull-through equal to those of the transabdominal pull-through?
expanded_query: Are the long-term results of the transanal pull-through equal to those of the transabdominal pull-through? Long Term Transanal approach (qualifier value) transanal pull-through transabdominal pull-through colorectal cancer colostomy abdominal surgery long-term results postoperative complications mortality quality of life
generated_answer: Reasoning: The study by Zhang et al. (2019) compared the long-term outcomes of the transanal endorectal pull-through (TERPT) and conventional transabdominal (ABD) pull-through for Hirschsprung disease (HD). The study examined 41 patients more than 3 years old who underwent a pull-through for HD (TERPT, n = 20; ABD, n = 21) [Context 17208539:1]. The study found that overall scores were similar. However, continence score was significantly better in the ABD group, and the stool pattern score was better in the TERPT group. The study also found that age at interview between the 2 groups was significantly different, and reanalyzing the data controlling for age showed that age did not significantly affect the long-term scoring outcome between groups.
raw_answer: Reasoning: The study by Zhang et al. (2019) compared the long-term outcomes of the transanal endorectal pull-through (TERPT) and conventional transabdominal (ABD) pull-through for Hirschsprung disease (HD). The study examined 41 patients more than 3 years old who underwent a pull-through for HD (TERPT, n = 20; ABD, n = 21). The study found that overall scores were similar. However, continence score was significantly better in the ABD group, and the stool pattern score was better in the TERPT group. The study also found that age at interview between the 2 groups was significantly different, and reanalyzing the data controlling for age showed that age did not significantly affect the long-term scoring outcome between groups.
reference_answer: Our long-term study showed significantly better (2-fold) results regarding the continence score for the abdominal approach compared with the transanal pull-through. The stool pattern and enterocolitis scores were somewhat better for the TERPT group. These findings raise an important issue about the current surgical management of HD; however, more cases will need to be studied before a definitive conclusion can be drawn.

The study examined 41 patients more than 3 years old who underwent a pull-through for HD (TERPT, n = 20; ABD, n = 21).
The study found that overall scores were similar.
However, continence score was significantly better in the ABD group, and the stool pattern score was better in the TERPT group.

The study also found that age at interview between the 2 groups was significantly different, and reanalyzing the data controlling for age showed that age did not significantly affect the long-term scoring outcome between groups.

question: Can tailored interventions increase mammography use among HMO women?
expanded_query: Can tailored interventions increase mammography use among HMO women? nursing intervention intervention Increase (qualifier value) Heightened Mammography (procedure) Health maintenance organization (environment) Organizations, Health Maintenance Human Females Girls health maintenance organization mammography breast neoplasms health behavior health promotion
generated_answer: Reasoning: The study by Katz et al. (1997) compared the effectiveness of tailored telephone counseling to tailored print materials for promoting mammography screening. The study found that compared to usual care, telephone counseling promoted a significantly higher proportion of women having mammograms on schedule (71% vs 61%) than did tailored print (67% vs 61%) [Context 10808977:5]. Furthermore, compared to usual care, telephone counseling was more effective than tailored print materials at promoting being on schedule with screening during 1996 and 1997 among women who were off-schedule during the previous year [Context 10808977:5].
raw_answer: Reasoning: The study by Katz et al. (1997) compared the effectiveness of tailored telephone counseling to tailored print materials for promoting mammography screening. The study found that compared to usual care, telephone counseling promoted a significantly higher proportion of women having mammograms on schedule (71% vs 61%) than did tailored print (67% vs 61%). Furthermore, compared to usual care, telephone counseling was more effective than tailored print materials at promoting being on schedule with screening during 1996 and 1997 among women who were off-schedule during the previous year.
reference_answer: The effects of the intervention were most pronounced after the first intervention. Compared to usual care, telephone counseling seemed particularly effective at promoting change among nonadherent women, the group for whom the intervention was developed. These results suggest that telephone counseling, rather than tailored print, might be the preferred first-line intervention for getting nonadherent women on schedule for mammography screening. Many questions would have to be answered about why the tailored print intervention was not more powerful. Nevertheless, it is clear that additional interventions will be needed to maintain women's adherence to mammography. Medical Subject Headings (MeSH): mammography screening, telephone counseling, tailored print communications, barriers.

Furthermore, compared to usual care, telephone counseling was more effective than tailored print materials at promoting being on schedule with screening during 1996 and 1997 among women who were off-schedule during the previous year.
